imageDescription: A child making a hand gesture near the nose, with the Luganda caption 'okutimba' visible above
notes: Image shows a hand-near-face gesture captioned 'okutimba' (a Luganda word unrelated to 'zero' or any obvious number concept). The gesture does not visibly match a 'Zero' handshape (e.g. an O-shape). Held for ambiguity: cannot confidently confirm 'Zero' nor confidently determine what the image actually depicts.

imageDescription: Person pointing with an extended index finger across the body
Person with both hands raised, palms facing out, curved arrows showing outward circular movement
Person with both hands extended forward/down, arrow showing curving movement

imageDescription: Person with both fists held near the chest, arrow showing upward movement
Person with index finger pointing up, arrow showing an arc movement above the head
Close-up of a hand with index finger touching near the nose/mouth, circular arrow showing movement
notes: Concrete number sign, label accurate. Acholi commonly uses the borrowed term 'miya' for hundred (as in many Luo/Bantu-contact varieties).

imageDescription: Close-up of a hand in a fist with index finger curling, pointing upward
notes: Concrete number sign, label accurate. 'Alufu' (from Swahili/Arabic 'elfu') is the commonly used Acholi word for 'thousand'.

imageDescription: Close-up of a hand with finger near the mouth, circular arrow showing movement
notes: Concrete number sign, label accurate. Existing acholiLabel 'labwor' actually means 'lion' (see glossary) and was incorrect for 'Million' -- replaced with the borrowed term 'miliyoni', which is how this modern/technical quantity is rendered in Acholi.

imageDescription: Close-up of a hand making a fist with index finger pointing upward
notes: Concrete number sign, label accurate. Existing acholiLabel 'labwor' actually means 'lion' (see glossary) and was incorrect for 'Billion' -- replaced with the borrowed term 'biliyoni'.

imageDescription: Close-up of two hands with fingertips touching, vertical double arrow showing movement
notes: Concrete ordinal-number gesture, label accurate. 'Mukwongo' is the standard Acholi word for 'first'.

imageDescription: Illustration of a boy making a hand sign near his nose, with the Luganda caption 'abazadde' (meaning 'parents') visible above
notes: Mislabeled 'Second' under numbers; image is actually a family-vocabulary sign captioned 'abazadde' (Luganda for 'parents'). Relabeled to 'Parents' and moved to the family category.

imageDescription: Illustration of a boy pointing with his hand, with the Luganda caption 'kojja' (meaning 'maternal uncle') visible above
notes: Mislabeled 'Third' under numbers; image is actually a family-vocabulary sign captioned 'kojja' (Luganda for 'maternal uncle'). Relabeled to 'Uncle' and moved to the family category. 'Nera' is the Acholi term for a mother's brother.

imageDescription: Illustration of a boy with a fist near his chest, with the Luganda caption 'ssenga' (meaning 'paternal aunt') visible above
notes: Mislabeled 'Fourth' under numbers; image is actually a family-vocabulary sign captioned 'ssenga' (Luganda for 'paternal aunt'). Relabeled to 'Aunt' and moved to the family category. 'Lamego' is the Acholi term for a father's sister.

imageDescription: Close-up of two hands with fingertips touching/pinching, double arrow showing upward movement
notes: Concrete ordinal-number gesture (same style as 'First'), label accurate. 'Mar abic' is a descriptive Acholi ordinal phrase for 'fifth'.

imageDescription: Person making a hand-crossing gesture near the chest, finger pointing
notes: Concrete number sign, label accurate. Acholi numbers 11-19 use the 'apar wiye N' (ten plus N) construction.

imageDescription: A cropped view of a person's back/torso with one arm, no hand or gesture visible
notes: Image is a poorly cropped fragment showing only a back/torso with no visible hand or sign content. No concrete sign for 'Number 12' can be justified; rejected as illegible.

imageDescription: Close-up of two hands with thumbs extended near each other, curved arrows showing circular movement
notes: Concrete number sign, label accurate. Acholi compound numbers use 'pyer-N gi M' (tens-N and M).
